title: First stone tools
desc: Early human ancestors in Africa began chipping stones into sharp tools for cutting and pounding.

title: Oldowan tool culture
desc: Early humans across Africa made simple chopper tools by knocking flakes off pebbles.

title: Homo erectus appears
desc: A tall, upright human ancestor appeared in Africa and later spread across much of Asia.

title: Humans reach Asia
desc: Homo erectus traveled out of Africa and lived in places like the country of Georgia and China.

title: Acheulean hand axes
desc: Early humans learned to shape teardrop-shaped stone hand axes used for many tasks.

title: Early control of fire
desc: Some early humans began using fire for warmth, cooking, and protection.

title: Regular use of fire
desc: By this time many groups of early humans used hearths and fire every day.

title: Neanderthals emerge
desc: Neanderthals, a tough cousin of modern humans, lived across Europe and western Asia.

title: Homo sapiens emerge
desc: Modern humans first appear in Africa, distinct from earlier hominid species.

title: First modern humans
desc: The earliest known Homo sapiens lived in Morocco, in northern Africa.

title: Middle Stone Age begins
desc: Humans in Africa made smaller, more refined stone tools and points for hunting.

title: Modern humans in Africa
desc: Early Homo sapiens were living and spreading across the continent of Africa.

title: Earliest shell beads
desc: Humans pierced sea shells to wear as some of the first known jewelry.

title: Toba super-eruption
desc: A giant volcano in Indonesia erupted, cooling the climate and challenging early humans.

title: Humans reach Australia
desc: People crossed the sea to Australia, becoming the ancestors of Aboriginal Australians.

title: Bow and arrow in Africa
desc: People at Sibudu Cave in South Africa may have used some of the first bows and arrows.

title: Out of Africa migration
desc: A big wave of modern humans left Africa and spread across Asia and beyond.

title: Oldest cave painting
desc: A painting of a wild pig in Sulawesi, Indonesia, is the oldest known animal art.

title: Humans reach Europe
desc: Modern humans moved into Europe, where Neanderthals already lived.
credit: Authors of the study: Kay Prüfer, Cosimo Posth, He Yu, Alexander Stoessel, Maria A. Spyrou, Thibaut Deviese, Marco Matto

title: Neanderthals die out
desc: The last Neanderthals disappeared, leaving modern humans as the only humans on Earth.

title: Chauvet Cave paintings
desc: Artists in France painted lions, rhinos, and horses deep inside Chauvet Cave.

title: Domestication of dogs
desc: Wolves began living alongside humans and slowly became the first tame dogs.

title: Oldest pottery objects
desc: Hunters in the Czech Republic shaped wet clay into animal figures and baked them hard.

title: Venus of Willendorf
desc: Someone in Austria carved a small, round stone figure of a woman.

title: Spear-thrower invented
desc: Hunters used a wooden lever called an atlatl to throw spears farther and harder.

title: Oldest known pottery
desc: People in southern China made some of the world's first clay pots for cooking.

title: Lascaux Cave paintings
desc: Artists in France covered cave walls with bulls, horses, and deer in glowing colors.

title: Altamira bison paintings
desc: Artists in Spain painted lifelike bison on the ceiling of Altamira Cave.
credit: Museo de Altamira y D. Rodríguez

title: Humans reach the Americas
desc: People crossed from Asia into Alaska and began spreading through the Americas.

title: Jomon pottery culture
desc: People in Japan made decorated clay pots, among the oldest pottery traditions.

title: Clovis hunters in America
desc: People across North America made distinctive spear points to hunt big animals.

title: Natufian villages
desc: People in the Middle East built some of the first permanent homes before farming began.

title: End of the Ice Age
desc: The last Ice Age ended as the climate warmed and great ice sheets melted away.

title: First wheat farming
desc: People in the Middle East began planting and harvesting wild wheat on purpose.

title: Agriculture begins
desc: Humans in the Fertile Crescent begin deliberately cultivating wheat and barley.

title: Gobekli Tepe built
desc: People in Turkey raised huge carved stone pillars to make the oldest known temple.

title: Jericho first settled
desc: One of the world's oldest towns grew up around a spring in the Jordan Valley.

title: Goats and sheep tamed
desc: People in the Middle East began herding goats and sheep for meat, milk, and wool.

title: Domestication of cattle
desc: Farmers tamed wild cattle, gaining a source of meat, milk, and muscle power.

title: Walls of Jericho
desc: The town of Jericho built a great stone wall and tower around itself.

title: Rice farming in China
desc: People along China's rivers began growing rice in wet fields.

title: Farming reaches everywhere
desc: The Neolithic Revolution spread as more people grew crops and kept animals.

title: Çatalhöyük thrives
desc: A densely populated Neolithic settlement in Anatolia houses thousands without streets or public squares.

title: Pottery invented
desc: Fired clay vessels appear independently in multiple regions, transforming food storage and cooking.

title: Maize farming begins
desc: People in Mexico began turning a wild grass called teosinte into corn.

title: Earliest copper use
desc: People in the Middle East hammered natural copper into beads and small tools.

title: First copper smelting
desc: People learned to melt copper out of rock, beginning the age of metals.

title: Irrigation farming
desc: Farmers in Mesopotamia dug channels to bring river water to their crops.

title: Sailing boats appear
desc: People in the Middle East began using sails to travel across water by wind.

title: First city of Eridu
desc: People in Mesopotamia built Eridu, one of the world's earliest cities.

title: Cotton grown for cloth
desc: People in South Asia and the Americas began growing cotton to make soft cloth.

title: Plows pulled by animals
desc: Farmers hitched oxen to wooden plows to turn the soil faster.

title: Llamas domesticated
desc: People in the Andes tamed llamas to carry loads and provide wool.

title: Copper Age in Europe
desc: People across Europe began making tools and ornaments out of copper.

title: Horses domesticated
desc: People on the grasslands of Central Asia began taming wild horses.
credit: Nokota_Horses.jpg: François Marchal derivative work: Dana boomer (talk)

title: Sahara turns to desert
desc: The once-green Sahara dried out, pushing people toward the Nile River valley.

title: The wheel invented
desc: People in Mesopotamia or Europe invented the wheel for carts and pottery.

title: Silk discovered in China
desc: People in China learned to unwind silkworm cocoons to make fine silk thread.
credit: Emperor Huizong of Song / After Zhang Xuan

title: Wheel invented
desc: The potter's wheel and wheeled vehicles appear in Mesopotamia and the Eurasian steppe.

title: Sumerian cities grow
desc: Large cities with temples rose across southern Mesopotamia.

title: Bronze Age begins
desc: The combination of copper and tin to make bronze spreads across Eurasia, transforming tools and weapons.

title: Otzi the Iceman
desc: A man crossing the Alps died and was frozen in ice with his copper axe for ages.

title: Bronze first made
desc: Metalworkers mixed copper and tin to make bronze, a harder and better metal.

title: Writing invented in Mesopotamia
desc: Sumerian scribes develop cuneiform, the earliest known writing system, to record trade and administration.

title: First writing appears
desc: People in Mesopotamia pressed marks into clay to keep records, inventing writing.

title: Sumerian Cuneiform Writing
desc: People in Sumer began pressing wedge-shaped marks into clay to record words and numbers.

title: Egypt unified
desc: Upper and Lower Egypt are brought together under a single pharaoh, founding the First Dynasty.

title: Unification of Egypt
desc: King Narmer joined Upper and Lower Egypt into one kingdom ruled by a single pharaoh.

title: City of Uruk Flourishes
desc: Uruk in Mesopotamia grew into one of the world's first big cities with thousands of people.

title: Egyptian Hieroglyphs Develop
desc: Ancient Egyptians wrote with picture symbols called hieroglyphs on stone and papyrus.

title: Stonehenge Building Begins
desc: People in Britain started building Stonehenge, a giant circle of standing stones.

title: Indus Valley Towns Grow
desc: Villages along the Indus River grew into planned towns with brick houses and drains.

title: First Egyptian Dynasty
desc: Egypt's earliest line of pharaohs began ruling from the city of Memphis.

title: Gilgamesh Rules Uruk
desc: A king named Gilgamesh ruled Uruk and later became the hero of famous stories.
credit: editor Austen Henry Layard , drawing by L. Gruner

title: Step Pyramid of Saqqara
desc: The architect Imhotep designed Egypt's first pyramid, built in giant stone steps.

title: Indus Valley civilization
desc: Sophisticated urban centers with planned streets and drainage systems flourish in the Indian subcontinent.

title: Great Pyramid built
desc: Pharaoh Khufu's tomb rises at Giza as one of the largest structures ever built by human hands.

title: Great Pyramid of Giza
desc: Pharaoh Khufu had the largest pyramid in Egypt built as his tomb.

title: Stonehenge constructed
desc: Massive standing stones are arranged on Salisbury Plain in a pattern aligned with the solstices.

title: The Great Sphinx Carved
desc: Egyptians carved a giant lion with a human head from solid rock near the pyramids.

title: Mohenjo-daro Built
desc: The Indus people built Mohenjo-daro, a city with straight streets and a great public bath.

title: Sumerian Mathematics
desc: Sumerians counted using a system based on the number sixty, which we still use for time.

title: Sargon Founds Akkad
desc: Sargon conquered the cities of Mesopotamia and built the world's first empire.

title: Akkadian Empire Spreads
desc: Sargon's empire stretched across Mesopotamia and used one common language for rule.

title: Enheduanna the Poet
desc: A princess named Enheduanna wrote hymns and became the first known author by name.

title: Code of Ur-Nammu
desc: King Ur-Nammu wrote one of the earliest known sets of written laws.

title: Epic of Gilgamesh Stories
desc: Storytellers spread tales of the hero Gilgamesh searching for everlasting life.

title: Xia Dynasty in China
desc: Chinese tradition says the Xia were China's first ruling dynasty along the Yellow River.

title: Minoan Palaces on Crete
desc: The Minoans built large palaces with colorful wall paintings on the island of Crete.

title: Domestication of the Horse
desc: People on the grasslands began riding and harnessing horses for travel and work.
credit: Nokota_Horses.jpg: François Marchal derivative work: Dana boomer (talk)

title: Abraham's Journey
desc: According to the Bible, Abraham left Mesopotamia and traveled to the land of Canaan.

title: Hittites Rise in Anatolia
desc: The Hittite people built a powerful kingdom in what is now Turkey.

title: Reign of Hammurabi
desc: Hammurabi became king of Babylon and conquered much of Mesopotamia.

title: Hammurabi's Code
desc: The Babylonian king inscribes one of history's earliest comprehensive legal codes on a stone stele.

title: Code of Hammurabi
desc: King Hammurabi carved a famous list of laws on a tall black stone pillar.

title: Mycenaean Greeks Rise
desc: Warrior kings built strong stone fortresses across mainland Greece.
credit: User:Alexikoua, User:Panthera tigris tigris, TL User:Reedside

title: Thera Volcano Erupts
desc: A huge volcano on the island of Thera exploded and reshaped the Aegean world.

title: Shang Dynasty Begins
desc: The Shang kings ruled northern China and made fine bronze vessels.

title: New Kingdom of Egypt
desc: Pharaoh Ahmose drove out the Hyksos and began Egypt's powerful New Kingdom.

title: Olmec Civilization Begins
desc: The Olmec people of Mexico built towns and carved giant stone heads.

title: Akhenaten's New Religion
desc: Pharaoh Akhenaten told Egypt to worship only the sun disk called the Aten.

title: Tutankhamun's Reign
desc: The boy king Tutankhamun restored Egypt's old gods before dying young.

title: Reign of Ramesses II
desc: Ramesses the Great ruled Egypt for many years and built huge temples.

title: Egypt-Hittite Peace Treaty
desc: Egypt and the Hittites signed one of the world's first known peace treaties.

title: Moses leads the Exodus
desc: According to the Hebrew scriptures, Moses leads the Israelites out of Egypt toward the Promised Land.

title: Trojan War
desc: Greek city-states lay siege to Troy in a conflict later immortalized in Homer's Iliad.
credit: Sosias (potter, signed). Painting attributed to the Sosias Painter (name piece for Beazley, overriding attribution) or t

title: Iron Age begins
desc: Iron-smelting technology spreads across the Old World, replacing bronze as the dominant metal.

title: Vedic Hymns in India
desc: Priests in India composed and chanted sacred Vedic hymns to their gods.

title: Bronze Age Collapse
desc: Many great kingdoms across the eastern Mediterranean suddenly fell apart.

title: Phoenician Alphabet
desc: Phoenician traders used a simple alphabet that became the root of many others.
credit: Matson Photo Service, photographer, published in 1936

title: Zhou Dynasty Begins
desc: The Zhou overthrew the Shang and began China's longest-lasting dynasty.

title: Mandate of Heaven Idea
desc: The Zhou said heaven gave good kings the right to rule and could take it away.

title: King David Rules Israel
desc: David united the Israelite tribes and made Jerusalem his capital city.

title: Reign of King Solomon
desc: Solomon ruled Israel in wealth and was remembered for his great wisdom.

title: Carthage Founded
desc: Phoenician settlers founded the trading city of Carthage in North Africa.

title: Greek Alphabet Adopted
desc: The Greeks added vowels to the Phoenician alphabet to write their language.

title: Greek City-States Form
desc: Independent Greek cities called poleis began to grow with their own governments.

title: First Olympic Games
desc: The Greeks held athletic games at Olympia to honor the god Zeus.

title: Rome founded
desc: According to Roman tradition, Romulus establishes the city on the banks of the Tiber River.

title: Founding of Rome
desc: Roman tradition says the city of Rome was founded by Romulus.

title: Homer composes the Iliad
desc: The epic poems attributed to Homer are set down, shaping Greek identity and literature for centuries.

title: Homer's Epics Composed
desc: Greek poets shaped the Iliad and Odyssey from old tales of heroes and war.

title: Greek Colonies Spread
desc: Greeks sailed out to found new colony cities around the Mediterranean and Black Sea.

title: Fall of the Kingdom of Israel
desc: Assyria conquered the northern kingdom of Israel and scattered its people.

title: Spartan Way of Life
desc: Sparta in Greece became a city focused almost entirely on training soldiers.

title: Coins First Made in Lydia
desc: The kingdom of Lydia struck the first true coins from a gold-silver metal.

title: Ashurbanipal's Library
desc: Assyrian king Ashurbanipal gathered thousands of clay tablets into a great library.

title: Neo-Babylonian Empire Rises
desc: Babylon broke free from Assyria and built a new powerful empire.

title: Draco's Harsh Laws
desc: The Athenian lawmaker Draco wrote strict written laws with very severe punishments.

title: Fall of Nineveh
desc: Babylonians and Medes destroyed Nineveh and ended the Assyrian Empire.

title: Reign of Nebuchadnezzar II
desc: Nebuchadnezzar II made Babylon the greatest city of its time.

title: Hanging Gardens of Babylon
desc: Babylon was said to have lush terraced gardens counted among the world's wonders.

title: Zoroaster's Teachings
desc: The Persian prophet Zoroaster taught about one great god and the fight of good and evil.

title: Solon Reforms Athens
desc: The wise leader Solon changed Athenian laws to ease the burden on the poor.

title: Babylonians Destroy Jerusalem
desc: Nebuchadnezzar's army destroyed Jerusalem and its temple.

title: Babylonian Exile of the Jews
desc: Many people of Judah were carried away to live as captives in Babylon.

title: Thales Predicts an Eclipse
desc: The Greek thinker Thales was said to foretell a solar eclipse, beginning science.

title: Buddha born
desc: Siddhartha Gautama is born in Lumbini, in what is now Nepal, the future founder of Buddhism.
credit: พระมหาเทวประภาส วชิรญาณเมธี (ผู้ถ่าย-ปล่อยสัญญาอนุญาตภาพให้นำไปใช้ได้เพื่อการศึกษาโดยอยู่ภา่ยใต้ cc-by-sa-3.0) ผู้สร้างส

title: Aesop's Fables Told
desc: Stories about clever animals with morals were linked to a storyteller named Aesop.

title: Confucius born
desc: Kong Qiu — later known as Confucius — is born in the state of Lu, in present-day Shandong, China.

title: Persian Empire founded
desc: Cyrus the Great unites the Medes and Persians, creating the largest empire the world had yet seen.

title: Cyrus the Great Founds Persia
desc: Cyrus united the Persians and Medes to start the huge Persian Empire.

title: Lao Tzu and Taoism
desc: A Chinese sage named Lao Tzu was linked to teachings about living in harmony.

title: Temple of Artemis at Ephesus
desc: A huge marble temple to the goddess Artemis was built at Ephesus.
credit: autor fotografie Zee Prime Original uploader was Zee Prime at cs.wikipedia

title: First Coins in the Greek World
desc: Greek cities spread the use of stamped metal coins for trade and payment.

title: Mahavira and Jainism
desc: The teacher Mahavira spread the Jain religion of nonviolence in India.

title: Cyrus Captures Babylon
desc: Cyrus the Great took the great city of Babylon without a long fight.

title: Cyrus the Great Frees the Jews
desc: The Persian king Cyrus let the Jewish people return home and rebuild their temple.

title: Persia Conquers Babylon
desc: Cyrus the Great captured the mighty city of Babylon for the Persian Empire.

title: Pythagoras Teaches Math
desc: The Greek thinker Pythagoras studied numbers, music, and triangles.

title: Pythagoras and His School
desc: The Greek thinker Pythagoras studied numbers and gave us a famous rule about triangles.

title: Buddha Reaches Enlightenment
desc: Prince Siddhartha became the Buddha after sitting under a tree seeking the truth.
credit: พระมหาเทวประภาส วชิรญาณเมธี (ผู้ถ่าย-ปล่อยสัญญาอนุญาตภาพให้นำไปใช้ได้เพื่อการศึกษาโดยอยู่ภา่ยใต้ cc-by-sa-3.0) ผู้สร้างส

title: Darius I Becomes King
desc: Darius I seized the throne and made the Persian Empire larger and better run.

title: Roman Republic Founded
desc: The Romans overthrew their last king and started a republic where leaders were elected.

title: Cleisthenes Reforms Athens
desc: An Athenian leader reorganized the city to give ordinary citizens more power, a big step toward democracy.

title: Democracy in Athens
desc: Cleisthenes introduces sweeping reforms that give Athenian male citizens direct political power.

title: Confucius Begins Teaching
desc: The Chinese philosopher Confucius traveled and taught ideas about kindness, family, and good government.

title: Sun Tzu's Art of War
desc: A Chinese general wrote a famous book of advice on strategy and warfare.

title: Lao Tzu and the Tao
desc: The Chinese sage Lao Tzu is credited with founding Taoism and writing the Tao Te Ching.

title: Battle of Marathon
desc: A smaller Athenian force defeats the Persian invasion, securing Greek independence.

title: Battle of Thermopylae
desc: King Leonidas and 300 Spartans made a famous last stand against the giant Persian army.

title: Battle of Salamis
desc: Greek ships crushed the larger Persian fleet in a narrow sea channel near Athens.
credit: The Department of History, United States Military Academy

title: Battle of Plataea
desc: A united Greek army defeated the Persians on land, ending Persia's invasion of Greece.

title: Socrates born
desc: The philosopher Socrates is born in Athens; his method of questioning will transform Western thought.

title: Twelve Tables of Rome
desc: Rome wrote down its laws on tablets so everyone could know the rules.

title: Age of Pericles
desc: Under the leader Pericles, Athens became a center of art, learning, and democracy.

title: Parthenon Construction Begins
desc: Athens started building the marble Parthenon temple to honor the goddess Athena.

title: Sophocles Writes Tragedies
desc: The Greek playwright Sophocles wrote famous plays like Oedipus the King.

title: Herodotus Writes Histories
desc: Herodotus, called the father of history, wrote about the wars between Greece and Persia.

title: Parthenon completed
desc: The iconic marble temple to Athena is finished on the Acropolis of Athens.

title: Peloponnesian War Begins
desc: Athens and Sparta started a long war for control of the Greek world.

title: Democritus and Atoms
desc: The Greek thinker Democritus guessed that everything is made of tiny atoms.

title: Hippocrates and Medicine
desc: The Greek doctor Hippocrates taught that diseases had natural causes, not magical ones.

title: Sparta Defeats Athens
desc: Sparta won the long Peloponnesian War, ending Athens' golden age of power.

title: Death of Socrates
desc: The Athenian philosopher Socrates was sentenced to death and drank poison hemlock.

title: Plato Founds the Academy
desc: The philosopher Plato started a school in Athens where people studied ideas and math.

title: Aristotle Tutors Alexander
desc: The great thinker Aristotle taught the young prince who became Alexander the Great.

title: Alexander Becomes King
desc: After his father's death, young Alexander the Great took the throne of Macedon.

title: Alexander Conquers Egypt
desc: Alexander the Great took Egypt and founded the city of Alexandria.

title: Battle of Gaugamela
desc: Alexander the Great smashed the huge Persian army and won control of the empire.

title: Alexander the Great conquers Persia
desc: Alexander of Macedon defeats Darius III and sweeps eastward, building an empire from Greece to India.

title: Death of Alexander
desc: Alexander the Great died young in Babylon after conquering a vast empire.

title: Chandragupta founds Maurya Empire
desc: Chandragupta Maurya unifies much of the Indian subcontinent under the first great pan-Indian empire.

title: Euclid writes Elements
desc: The Greek mathematician lays out the foundations of geometry in a systematic work studied for two millennia.
credit: Jusepe de Ribera (Spanish / Italian, 1591 - 1652) (1591 - 1652) – artist (Spanish / Italian) Details on Google Art Proje

title: Euclid Writes the Elements
desc: The mathematician Euclid wrote a famous geometry book still studied today.
credit: Jusepe de Ribera (Spanish / Italian, 1591 - 1652) (1591 - 1652) – artist (Spanish / Italian) Details on Google Art Proje

title: Library of Alexandria Founded
desc: Egypt created a huge library that tried to collect all the world's knowledge.

title: Lighthouse of Alexandria Built
desc: Egypt built a giant lighthouse, one of the Seven Wonders of the ancient world.

title: Aristarchus Suggests Sun Center
desc: A Greek astronomer guessed that Earth moves around the Sun, long before others believed it.

title: First Punic War Begins
desc: Rome and Carthage started the first of three great wars over the Mediterranean.

title: Ashoka converts to Buddhism
desc: After a bloody conquest, Mauryan Emperor Ashoka embraces Buddhism and spreads its principles across his empire.

title: Ashoka Embraces Buddhism
desc: The Indian emperor Ashoka felt sorrow after a bloody war and spread peaceful Buddhist teachings.

title: Archimedes' Discoveries
desc: The Greek genius Archimedes invented machines and discovered laws of floating and levers.

title: Maya Cities Begin to Grow
desc: In Central America, the Maya started building early towns and pyramids.

title: Eratosthenes Measures Earth
desc: A Greek scholar cleverly calculated the size of the round Earth using shadows.

title: Qin Shi Huang unifies China
desc: The king of Qin conquers the warring states and declares himself First Emperor of a unified China.

title: Qin Unifies China
desc: The ruler of Qin conquered the warring states and became China's first emperor.

title: Hannibal crosses the Alps
desc: The Carthaginian general leads his army, including war elephants, over the Alps to invade Roman Italy.

title: Battle of Cannae
desc: Hannibal surrounded and destroyed a much larger Roman army in his greatest victory.

title: Great Wall Linked Together
desc: China's first emperor connected older walls into one long Great Wall for defense.

title: Terracotta Army Created
desc: China's first emperor was buried with thousands of life-sized clay soldiers to guard him.

title: Han Dynasty established
desc: Liu Bang defeats his rivals and founds the Han Dynasty, one of China's most celebrated imperial periods.

title: Han Dynasty Begins
desc: After the Qin fell, the long-lasting Han dynasty took control of China.

title: Battle of Zama
desc: The Roman general Scipio defeated Hannibal, ending the Second Punic War.

title: Rosetta Stone Carved
desc: Egyptians carved a stone in three scripts that later helped scholars read hieroglyphs.

title: Rome Destroys Carthage
desc: Rome won the Third Punic War and burned its great rival city Carthage to the ground.

title: Rome Conquers Greece
desc: Rome defeated the Greek cities and made Greece part of its growing empire.
credit: Drawn by F. W. Putzger, A. Baldamus and E. Schwabe, uploaded to Wikimedia Commons by User:Gyalu22

title: Silk Road opens
desc: Trade routes linking China to Central Asia and the Mediterranean begin carrying silk, spices, and ideas.

title: Spartacus Slave Revolt
desc: An enslaved gladiator named Spartacus led a huge rebellion against Rome.

title: Caesar Invades Britain
desc: Julius Caesar led the first Roman expeditions across the sea to Britain.

title: Caesar Conquers Gaul
desc: Julius Caesar spent years conquering Gaul, today's France, for Rome.

title: Caesar Crosses the Rubicon
desc: Julius Caesar led his army across a river into Italy, starting a civil war.

title: Cleopatra Meets Caesar
desc: Egypt's queen Cleopatra allied with Julius Caesar to win her throne.

title: Julian Calendar Begins
desc: Julius Caesar created a new calendar with 365 days and leap years.
credit: Ángel M. Felicísimo from Mérida, España

title: Julius Caesar assassinated
desc: Senators led by Brutus and Cassius stab Caesar on the Ides of March, ending his dictatorship.

title: Assassination of Caesar
desc: Roman senators stabbed Julius Caesar to death on the Ides of March.

title: Battle of Actium
desc: Octavian defeated Mark Antony and Cleopatra in a great sea battle.

title: Death of Cleopatra
desc: Egypt's last pharaoh, Cleopatra, died and Egypt became part of Rome.

title: Augustus becomes first Roman Emperor
desc: Octavian takes the title Augustus, transforming the Roman Republic into an empire.

title: Augustus Becomes Emperor
desc: Octavian took the name Augustus and became Rome's first emperor.

title: Pax Romana Begins
desc: Augustus started a long period of peace and prosperity across the Roman Empire.

title: Virgil Writes the Aeneid
desc: The Roman poet Virgil wrote an epic poem about the legendary founding of Rome.

title: Jesus of Nazareth born
desc: Jesus, whose life and teachings become the foundation of Christianity, is born in Judea.

title: Birth of Jesus
desc: Jesus of Nazareth, the central figure of Christianity, was born in Judea.

title: Battle of Teutoburg Forest
desc: German tribes ambushed and destroyed three Roman legions in a dark forest.

title: Jesus crucified
desc: Jesus is executed by Roman crucifixion; his followers proclaim his resurrection and establish the early church.

title: Crucifixion of Jesus
desc: Jesus was crucified in Jerusalem, an event central to the Christian faith.

title: Roman Invasion of Britain
desc: Emperor Claudius sent armies to conquer the island of Britain for Rome.

title: Paul's Missionary Journeys
desc: The apostle Paul traveled the Roman world spreading the Christian message.

title: Jewish Revolt Against Rome
desc: The Jewish people of Judea rose up in a major rebellion against Roman rule.
credit: derivative work: Steerpike (talk) Arc_de_Triumph_copy.jpg: user: בית השלום

title: Buddhism Reaches China
desc: Buddhist ideas and monks first traveled along the Silk Road into China.

title: Destruction of the Temple
desc: Roman soldiers captured Jerusalem and destroyed the great Jewish Temple.

title: Vesuvius erupts, buries Pompeii
desc: Mount Vesuvius erupts catastrophically, entombing the Roman city of Pompeii in ash and preserving it.

title: Eruption of Vesuvius
desc: The volcano Vesuvius erupted and buried the Roman towns of Pompeii and Herculaneum.

title: Colosseum opens in Rome
desc: The massive amphitheater is inaugurated with a hundred days of games, seating up to 80,000 spectators.

title: Colosseum Opens
desc: Rome opened its giant arena where crowds watched gladiator games and shows.

title: Teotihuacan built
desc: A massive planned city rises in central Mexico, featuring the Pyramid of the Sun and influencing cultures for centuries.

title: Nazca Lines Drawn
desc: People in Peru carved giant pictures of animals into the desert ground.

title: Cai Lun Improves Paper
desc: A Chinese official perfected papermaking, making cheap writing material widely available.

title: Trajan Expands the Empire
desc: Emperor Trajan pushed Rome to its largest size, conquering Dacia and lands in the east.

title: Hadrian's Wall Built
desc: The Romans built a long stone wall across northern Britain to keep out raiders.
credit: Hadrians_Wall_map.png: Created by NormanEinstein, September 20, 2005 derivative work: Talifero (talk)

title: Pantheon Rebuilt in Rome
desc: Emperor Hadrian rebuilt the Pantheon temple with its famous huge concrete dome.

title: Ptolemy's Almagest
desc: The scholar Ptolemy wrote a famous book placing Earth at the center of the universe.

title: Marcus Aurelius Writes Meditations
desc: The philosopher-emperor Marcus Aurelius wrote private notes on how to live wisely.

title: Galen Studies the Body
desc: The Greek doctor Galen made discoveries about anatomy that guided medicine for centuries.

title: Maya Build Tikal

title: Partition of India and Pakistan
desc: The end of British rule splits the subcontinent into two nations, triggering one of history's largest mass migrations.

title: India gains independence
desc: India became independent from British rule and split into India and Pakistan.
credit: British Information Services, an agency of the British Government, restoration by Wilfredor

title: Transistor invented
desc: Scientists at Bell Labs invented the transistor, the tiny building block of modern electronics.

title: State of Israel established
desc: Jewish leaders declare the State of Israel, immediately triggering war with neighboring Arab states.

title: State of Israel founded
desc: The modern nation of Israel was created in the Middle East.

title: Gandhi assassinated
desc: Mahatma Gandhi, leader of India's peaceful independence movement, was killed.

title: Apartheid begins in South Africa
desc: South Africa began a system of laws that separated people by race called apartheid.

title: People's Republic of China proclaimed
desc: Mao Zedong proclaims the founding of the PRC after the Communist Party defeats Nationalist forces.

title: People's Republic of China founded
desc: Mao Zedong declared the founding of the People's Republic of China.

title: NATO alliance formed
desc: Western countries formed the NATO military alliance for mutual defense.

title: George Orwell's 1984 published
desc: Author George Orwell published his famous novel about a controlling future government.

title: Korean War begins
desc: North Korea invades South Korea, drawing in the United States and ultimately China in a three-year conflict.
credit: Photo by Corporal Peter McDonald, USMC

title: Korean War armistice signed
desc: After three years of fighting, an armistice halts combat on the Korean Peninsula, leaving the country divided.
credit: U.S. Department of Defense (F. Kazukaitis. U.S. Navy)

title: DNA structure discovered
desc: Scientists Watson and Crick described the double helix shape of DNA.

title: Mount Everest first climbed
desc: Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay became the first to reach the top of the world's highest mountain.

title: Brown v. Board of Education
desc: The U.S. Supreme Court unanimously rules that racial segregation in public schools is unconstitutional.
credit: NYWT&S staff photo by Al Ravenna.

title: Polio vaccine developed
desc: Jonas Salk created a vaccine that protected children from the disease polio.

title: Rosa Parks bus protest
desc: Rosa Parks refused to give up her bus seat, sparking a civil rights protest in Alabama.

title: Suez Crisis
desc: A conflict over the Suez Canal in Egypt drew in several major nations.

title: Sputnik launched
desc: The Soviet Union launches the world's first artificial satellite, triggering the space race with the United States.

title: Ghana gains independence
desc: Ghana became the first sub-Saharan African country to gain independence from Europe.

title: Treaty of Rome signed
desc: European countries signed a treaty creating the European Economic Community.
credit: User:Verdy p, User:-xfi-, User:Paddu, User:Nightstallion, User:Funakoshi, User:Jeltz, User:Dbenbenn, User:Zscout370

title: NASA created
desc: The United States founded NASA to lead its space exploration efforts.
credit: The original uploader was Vargklo at English Wikipedia.

title: Integrated circuit invented
desc: Engineers invented the integrated circuit, the basis of modern computer chips.

title: Cuban Revolution succeeds
desc: Fidel Castro's rebels took power in Cuba, overthrowing the government.

title: First working laser
desc: Scientist Theodore Maiman built the first working laser device.

title: Year of Africa
desc: Seventeen African nations gained independence from European rule in a single year.

title: First human in space
desc: Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin became the first person to travel into space.

title: Berlin Wall built
desc: East Germany built a wall through Berlin to stop people from fleeing to the West.

title: Cuban Missile Crisis
desc: The U.S. and Soviet Union come to the brink of nuclear war over Soviet missiles in Cuba before negotiating a resolution.

title: First American orbits Earth
desc: Astronaut John Glenn became the first American to circle the Earth in space.

title: Silent Spring published
desc: Rachel Carson's book Silent Spring warned about the dangers of pesticides to nature.

title: Kennedy assassinated
desc: President John F. Kennedy is shot and killed in Dallas, Texas, shocking the nation.

title: Martin Luther King Jr.'s 'I Have a Dream' speech
desc: King delivers his landmark address to 250,000 people in Washington, D.C., calling for racial equality.

title: First woman in space
desc: Soviet cosmonaut Valentina Tereshkova became the first woman to fly in space.
credit: This file comes from the website of the President of the Russian Federation and is licensed under the Creative Commons A

title: March on Washington
desc: Martin Luther King Jr. gave his I Have a Dream speech to a huge crowd in Washington.
credit: This photograph was made by Rowland Scherman at the March on Washington. The negatives are in the custody of the Nationa

title: Civil Rights Act passed
desc: U.S. Congress passes landmark legislation outlawing discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.

title: US Civil Rights Act passed
desc: A landmark law banned discrimination based on race in the United States.

title: First spacewalk
desc: Soviet cosmonaut Alexei Leonov became the first person to walk in space outside a spacecraft.
credit: This image or video was catalogued by Lyndon B. Johnson Space Center of the United States National Aeronautics and Space

title: Voting Rights Act passed
desc: A US law protected the voting rights of Black Americans.

title: Cultural Revolution in China
desc: Mao Zedong launched the Cultural Revolution, causing major upheaval in China.

title: First heart transplant
desc: Surgeon Christiaan Barnard performed the first human heart transplant in South Africa.

title: Six-Day War
desc: Israel fought and won a brief war against neighboring Arab countries.

title: Martin Luther King assassinated
desc: Civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. was killed in Memphis, Tennessee.

title: First crewed flight around Moon
desc: Apollo 8 astronauts became the first humans to orbit the Moon.

title: Prague Spring
desc: A movement for freedom in Czechoslovakia was crushed by Soviet tanks.

title: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon
desc: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin become the first humans to walk on the Moon, fulfilling Kennedy's challenge.

title: First Moon landing
desc: Apollo 11 astronaut Neil Armstrong became the first person to walk on the Moon.

title: Internet's first message
desc: The first message was sent over ARPANET, an early version of the internet.

title: First space station launched
desc: The Soviet Union launched Salyut 1, the first space station to orbit Earth.

title: Bangladesh gains independence
desc: Bangladesh became an independent country after a war with Pakistan.

title: Intel's first microprocessor
desc: Intel released the first microprocessor, putting a computer's brain on a single chip.

title: First handheld mobile phone call
desc: An engineer at Motorola made the first call on a handheld mobile phone.

title: Lucy fossil discovered
desc: Scientists in Ethiopia found Lucy, a famous ancient human ancestor skeleton.

title: Terracotta Army discovered
desc: Farmers in China uncovered thousands of life-sized clay soldiers buried with an emperor.

title: Vietnam War ends
desc: North Vietnamese forces capture Saigon, ending the Vietnam War and reunifying the country under communist rule.

title: Microsoft founded
desc: Bill Gates and Paul Allen started the software company Microsoft.

title: Apple founded
desc: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak founded Apple to make personal computers.

title: Viking lands on Mars
desc: NASA's Viking 1 became the first US spacecraft to land successfully on Mars.

title: Voyager probes launched
desc: NASA launched two Voyager probes to explore the outer planets of the solar system.

title: First test-tube baby
desc: Louise Brown became the first baby born using in vitro fertilization.

title: Iranian Revolution
desc: The Shah of Iran is overthrown, and Ayatollah Khomeini establishes an Islamic Republic.

title: Smallpox eradicated
desc: The World Health Organization declared smallpox wiped out worldwide after a vaccination effort.
credit: Photo Credit: Content Providers(s): CDC/James Hicks

title: First space shuttle flight
desc: NASA launched the space shuttle Columbia, the first reusable spacecraft.

title: IBM personal computer released
desc: IBM launched its personal computer, helping bring PCs into homes and offices.
credit: Own work (based on graphics by International Business Machines Corporation)

title: AIDS first identified
desc: Doctors identified AIDS, a serious disease caused by the HIV virus.

title: Apple Macintosh released
desc: Apple introduced the Macintosh, a computer with a friendly point-and-click design.

title: DNA fingerprinting developed
desc: Scientist Alec Jeffreys invented a way to identify people using their unique DNA.

title: Ozone hole discovered
desc: Scientists found a large hole in the ozone layer above Antarctica.

title: Chernobyl disaster
desc: A nuclear power plant in Ukraine exploded, spreading dangerous radiation.

title: Challenger shuttle disaster
desc: The space shuttle Challenger broke apart shortly after launch, killing its crew.

title: Berlin Wall falls
desc: East Germany opens its borders; jubilant crowds tear down the Wall, heralding the end of the Cold War.
credit: Unknown photographer, Reproduction by Lear 21 at English Wikipedia.

title: Tiananmen Square protests
desc: Pro-democracy protesters occupy Beijing's Tiananmen Square before the Chinese military violently suppresses the movement.

title: World Wide Web invented
desc: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web, making the internet easy to use.

title: Hubble Space Telescope launched
desc: NASA launched the Hubble Space Telescope to take amazing pictures of distant space.

title: Nelson Mandela freed
desc: Anti-apartheid leader Nelson Mandela was released after 27 years in a South African prison.

title: Germany reunified
desc: East and West Germany joined back together into one country.
credit: Unknown photographer, Reproduction by Lear 21 at English Wikipedia.

title: Soviet Union dissolves
desc: The USSR officially ceases to exist, breaking into fifteen independent states and ending the Cold War era.
credit: Special:Contributions/Saul ip. Derivative work by Σ (talk)

title: World Wide Web goes public
desc: Tim Berners-Lee releases the World Wide Web to the public, transforming global communication and commerce.

title: Gulf War
desc: A coalition of countries fought to push Iraq out of Kuwait.

title: Maastricht Treaty signed
desc: European countries signed a treaty that created the European Union.

title: Nelson Mandela elected president
desc: After 27 years in prison, Mandela is elected South Africa's first Black president in its first fully democratic election.

title: Rwandan Genocide
desc: In 100 days, extremist Hutu militias massacre approximately 800,000 Tutsi and moderate Hutu in Rwanda.

title: Mandela becomes president
desc: Nelson Mandela was elected South Africa's first Black president in a free election.

title: Nelson Mandela ends apartheid era
desc: South Africa held its first election open to all races, ending apartheid.

title: Amazon online store founded
desc: Jeff Bezos started Amazon as an online bookstore.
credit: U.S. Department of Defense photo by Senior Master Sgt. Adrian Cadiz

title: Dolly the sheep cloned
desc: Scottish scientists announce the successful cloning of a sheep from an adult cell, a first in history.

title: Hong Kong returned to China
desc: Britain transfers sovereignty over Hong Kong to the People's Republic of China after 156 years of colonial rule.

title: Deep Blue beats chess champion
desc: An IBM computer named Deep Blue beat world chess champion Garry Kasparov.

title: Hong Kong returns to China
desc: Britain handed control of Hong Kong back to China.

title: Google founded
desc: Larry Page and Sergey Brin started the search engine company Google.

title: International Space Station begins
desc: The first pieces of the International Space Station were launched and joined in orbit.

title: Good Friday Agreement
desc: A peace deal helped end decades of conflict in Northern Ireland.
credit: The original uploader was Jonto at English Wikipedia.

title: Euro currency introduced
desc: Many European countries adopted the euro as a shared new currency.

title: Human Genome First Draft
desc: Scientists announced they had mapped a rough draft of the human genome, the full set of instructions inside our cells.
credit: U.S. Department of Energy, Human Genome Project

title: First Crew on Space Station
desc: The first astronauts and cosmonauts moved into the International Space Station to begin living and working in orbit.

title: Vladimir Putin Becomes President
desc: Vladimir Putin was first elected president of Russia, beginning his long role as the country's leader.

title: September 11 attacks
desc: Al-Qaeda terrorists hijack four airplanes and kill nearly 3,000 people in attacks on the U.S., triggering the War on Terror.

title: Wikipedia Launched
desc: Wikipedia began as a free online encyclopedia that anyone in the world could help write and edit.
credit: Version 1 by Nohat (concept by Paullusmagnus); Wikimedia.

title: Apple Releases the iPod
desc: Apple introduced the iPod, a small device that let people carry thousands of songs in their pocket.

title: Euro Banknotes and Coins
desc: Euro coins and notes began being used as everyday cash across many countries in Europe.

title: Iraq War begins
desc: A U.S.-led coalition invades Iraq, toppling Saddam Hussein's government.
credit: Technical Sergeant John L. Houghton, Jr., United States Air Force

title: Human Genome Completed
desc: Scientists finished reading the human genome, a huge achievement for medicine and biology.
credit: U.S. Department of Energy, Human Genome Project

title: iTunes Store Opens
desc: Apple opened the iTunes Store, letting people legally buy and download single songs over the internet.

title: Indian Ocean tsunami
desc: A massive undersea earthquake triggers a tsunami that kills over 230,000 people across fourteen countries.
credit: U.S. Navy photo by Photographer's Mate 2nd Class Philip A. McDaniel

title: Facebook Founded
desc: Facebook began at a college and grew into one of the world's largest social networks.

title: Mars Rovers Land
desc: NASA's twin rovers Spirit and Opportunity landed on Mars to explore the red planet's surface.

title: First Private Spaceflight
desc: SpaceShipOne became the first privately built craft to carry a pilot to the edge of space.

title: European Union Grows
desc: Ten new countries joined the European Union in its largest expansion ever.
credit: User:Verdy p, User:-xfi-, User:Paddu, User:Nightstallion, User:Funakoshi, User:Jeltz, User:Dbenbenn, User:Zscout370

title: YouTube Founded
desc: YouTube was created as a website where anyone could upload and share videos with the world.
credit: Lyles BBQ, Smile and Learn - English, The Flat Top King (previous screenshot: yumyotta)

title: Hurricane Katrina
desc: Hurricane Katrina caused severe flooding in New Orleans and along the United States Gulf Coast.

title: Kyoto Protocol Takes Effect
desc: An international agreement to reduce greenhouse gases officially came into force for many nations.

title: Twitter Launches
desc: Twitter began as a service for sharing very short messages, called tweets, with followers.

title: Pluto Reclassified
desc: Astronomers decided to call Pluto a dwarf planet, leaving our solar system with eight major planets.
credit: NASA/Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory/Southwest Research Institute/Alex Parker

title: First iPhone Released
desc: Apple released the first iPhone, a touchscreen phone that helped start the smartphone age.

title: Barack Obama elected president
desc: Obama becomes the first African American president of the United States.
credit: Official White House Photo by Pete Souza

title: Barack Obama Elected
desc: Barack Obama was elected as the first Black president of the United States.
credit: Official White House Photo by Pete Souza

title: Global Financial Crisis
desc: Banks and stock markets around the world fell sharply, leading to tough economic times for many people.

title: Large Hadron Collider Starts
desc: Europe's giant Large Hadron Collider switched on to smash tiny particles and study how the universe works.

title: First Android Phone
desc: The first phone using Google's Android software went on sale, giving the iPhone a major rival.

title: Bitcoin Idea Published
desc: A mysterious author shared a paper describing Bitcoin, a new kind of digital money.

title: H1N1 Flu Pandemic
desc: A new strain of flu known as swine flu spread worldwide, leading to a global health response.

title: Bitcoin Network Begins
desc: The Bitcoin network started running, allowing the first digital coins to be created and sent.

title: Arab Spring begins
desc: Pro-democracy protests sweep across the Arab world, toppling governments in Tunisia, Egypt, Libya, and Yemen.

title: Apple Releases the iPad
desc: Apple introduced the iPad, a flat touchscreen tablet that became very popular for reading and games.

title: Osama bin Laden killed
desc: U.S. Navy SEALs raid a compound in Pakistan and kill al-Qaeda leader Osama bin Laden.

title: Japan Earthquake and Tsunami
desc: A huge earthquake and tsunami hit Japan and damaged the Fukushima nuclear power plant.

title: South Sudan Becomes a Country
desc: South Sudan declared independence and became the world's newest nation.

title: Final Space Shuttle Flight
desc: NASA flew its last space shuttle mission, ending thirty years of the famous winged spacecraft.

title: World Reaches 7 Billion
desc: The number of people living on Earth passed seven billion for the first time.

title: Curiosity Lands on Mars
desc: NASA's car-sized Curiosity rover landed on Mars to study whether the planet could ever support life.

title: Higgs Boson Discovered
desc: Scientists found the Higgs boson, a particle that helps explain why other particles have mass.
credit: CERN for the ATLAS and CMS Collaborations

title: Dragon Reaches Space Station
desc: A SpaceX Dragon became the first private spacecraft to deliver cargo to the International Space Station.
credit: Original: NASA Johnson Space Center Derivative work: Nythar

title: Pope Francis Elected
desc: Cardinal Jorge Bergoglio of Argentina was chosen as Pope Francis, the first pope from the Americas.
credit: Korea.net / Korean Culture and Information Service (Jeon Han)

title: Voyager 1 Enters Interstellar Space
desc: NASA confirmed that Voyager 1 had become the first spacecraft to travel beyond our solar system.

title: Nelson Mandela Dies
desc: The world mourned Nelson Mandela, the leader who helped end apartheid in South Africa.

title: Landing on a Comet
desc: Europe's Philae probe became the first spacecraft to land on a comet far out in space.

title: Ebola Outbreak in West Africa
desc: A serious outbreak of the Ebola virus in West Africa led to a major international health effort.

title: Malala Wins Nobel Prize
desc: Malala Yousafzai of Pakistan won the Nobel Peace Prize for defending girls' right to education.

title: Same-sex marriage legalized in U.S.
desc: The Supreme Court rules in Obergefell v. Hodges that the Constitution guarantees same-sex couples the right to marry.

title: Paris Climate Agreement
desc: Nearly every country agreed in Paris to work together to limit global warming.

title: New Horizons Flies Past Pluto
desc: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft flew past Pluto and sent back the first close-up photos of the dwarf planet.
credit: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), Applied Physics Laboratory

title: Same-Sex Marriage Nationwide
desc: The United States Supreme Court ruled that same-sex couples could marry in every state.

title: Flowing Water Found on Mars
desc: Scientists announced signs that liquid water may sometimes flow on the surface of Mars.

title: Brexit Referendum
desc: People in the United Kingdom voted to leave the European Union in a vote called Brexit.
credit: Mirrorme22 Brythones Nilfanion: English and Scottish council areas TUBS: Welsh council areas Sting: Gibraltar

title: Donald Trump Elected
desc: Businessman Donald Trump was elected president of the United States.

title: Gravitational Waves Detected
desc: Scientists detected gravitational waves, tiny ripples in space first predicted by Albert Einstein.
credit: Simulating eXtreme Spacetimes Collaboration/Canadian Institute for Theoretical Astrophysics/SciNet

title: Computer Masters the Game of Go
desc: The AlphaGo program beat a top human champion at the ancient and complicated board game Go.

title: Juno Reaches Jupiter
desc: NASA's Juno spacecraft arrived at Jupiter to study the largest planet in our solar system.

title: Total Eclipse Crosses America
desc: A total solar eclipse swept across the United States as the Moon blocked out the Sun.

title: Cassini Ends Its Mission
desc: After many years of discovery, the Cassini spacecraft dived into Saturn to finish its journey.
credit: Kevin Gill from Los Angeles, CA, United States

title: First Image of a Black Hole
desc: Scientists revealed the first-ever photograph of a black hole, captured by telescopes around the world.
credit: Event Horizon Telescope, uploader cropped and converted TIF to JPG

title: Notre-Dame Cathedral Fire
desc: A fire badly damaged the famous Notre-Dame cathedral in Paris, sparking a worldwide effort to rebuild it.

title: Global Climate Strikes
desc: Millions of young people, inspired by activist Greta Thunberg, marched to demand action on climate change.

title: COVID-19 pandemic begins
desc: A novel coronavirus spreads from China across the globe, triggering the worst pandemic in a century.

title: United Kingdom Leaves the EU
desc: The United Kingdom officially left the European Union, completing the process known as Brexit.

title: Helicopter Flies on Mars
desc: NASA's tiny Ingenuity helicopter made the first powered flight on another planet.

title: James Webb Telescope Launched
desc: The powerful James Webb Space Telescope launched on a mission to see the earliest stars and galaxies.

title: First Webb Telescope Images
desc: The James Webb Space Telescope shared its first dazzling images of distant galaxies and nebulae.

title: Russia Invades Ukraine
desc: Russia launched a large invasion of neighboring Ukraine, beginning a major war in Europe.

title: Queen Elizabeth II Dies
desc: Queen Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom passed away after the longest reign in British history.

title: ChatGPT Released
desc: A chatbot called ChatGPT launched and showed how powerful and popular artificial intelligence could be.

title: India Lands on the Moon
desc: India's Chandrayaan-3 mission landed near the Moon's south pole, a first for any country.

title: India Most Populous Nation
desc: India passed China to become the most populous country in the world.
